ProjectsPage.bio: I am a full-stack developer focused on building web services that users can intuitively understand and conveniently use. I design and ship interfaces with React.js and Next.js, and connect them to APIs, databases, and server-side logic with Node.js to solve problems and deliver polished services. My goal is to grow into a developer who not only implements features but also maximizes the value of the user experience.

ProjectsPage.coreBeliefTitle: C O R E  B E L I E F S

coreBeliefsList.title: Continuous Growth
coreBeliefsList.description: Technology and design are constantly evolving. I strive to keep learning, improving, and creating better outcomes without ever standing still.

coreBeliefsList.title: User-Centered Mindset
coreBeliefsList.description: Every project begins with the user. I design and build with empathy, aiming to deliver intuitive and meaningful experiences.

coreBeliefsList.title: Collaboration & Trust
coreBeliefsList.description: Great services are not built alone but together. I value open collaboration and believe trust is the foundation for achieving shared goals.

ProjectsPage.interviewTitle: I N T E R V I E W

interviewsList.title: What led you to pursue full-stack development?
interviewsList.description: I started with C and Java, but found it hard to stay motivated when results did not surface immediately. The web was different: code quickly becomes something you can see and interact with. Through clone projects and structured study, I moved from the client layer toward server integration—how data should be modeled, exposed through APIs, and experienced through the UI. I joined a full-stack bootcamp and, across several team projects, felt the moment when databases, backends, and frontends clicked into one pipeline. I get energy from owning the thread from data modeling to user experience, not from staying inside a single layer—that is why I pursue work as a full-stack developer.

interviewsList.title: What is your greatest strength as a full-stack developer?
interviewsList.description: I can hold both the architecture behind the screen and the experience in front of it. When I build UI/UX with a clear picture of APIs and data flow, I reduce redundant round-trips, inconsistent state, and gaps between design and implementation. On team projects I often sit at the boundary between stacks—aligning collaboration and, in code review, checking what users touch and what the system relies on. My edge is not listing technologies; it is closing the layers myself so a feature ships end-to-end with clarity—that is the trust I aim to earn.

interviewsList.title: What efforts have you made for self-development?
interviewsList.description: After online courses and a bootcamp, I drilled CRUD, REST APIs, and ORMs until server and data work felt familiar, while on the frontend I refined component structure and state management in tandem. When a new framework or pattern appears, I validate it through docs and small spikes first, then unblock with feedback from experienced developers. Full-stack breadth is wide, so I deepen one layer at a time without losing sight of the overall architecture—that habit is how I keep growing.
